summary: Julia Evans briefly documents her decision to switch from Jekyll to Octopress for her blog. She found Jekyll's HTML, CSS, and layout configuration tedious, and Octopress offered an easier setup experience. The post is essentially a first impression noting that things look okay so far. Her main remaining concern is customizing the color scheme to be more orange.
key_points: Jekyll's manual HTML/CSS/layout setup was frustrating enough to motivate switching tools
Octopress offered a significantly easier setup experience out of the box
Customization (making it 'more orange') remains a priority even with the simpler tool
money_quotes: Decided that I was tired of messing with HTML and CSS and layouts to set up Jekyll, so trying out Octopress here instead.
Still need to figure how to make it more orange.
key_insight: Sometimes the best tool is the one that gets out of your way and lets you focus on what you actually care about.
